title: Sort Semantics, Group Labels & Live Region Fixes
description: Fix a set of missing ARIA semantics: wire aria-describedby in WeeklyGoalSection to its error paragraph, add role='group' to DashboardToolbar view toggle, replace raw ↑↓ sort arrows with sr-only text in ApplicationListHeader, add aria-hidden to PublicTimeline decorative timeline dots/lines/separators, remove redundant aria-label from PublicTimeline StagePill, add sr-only met/not-met state to RegisterForm password requirements, and add aria-busy to DashboardToolbar Export button.

userStories.title: Add aria-describedby to WeeklyGoalSection Input for error paragraph
userStories.description:
WeeklyGoalSection.jsx renders a validation error in a

(~line 22) but that paragraph has no id, so the Input has no aria-describedby. Screen reader users hear the role='alert' but the Input still lacks a programmatic error association. Add id='goal-error' to the error

and add aria-describedby={goalError ? 'goal-error' : undefined} to the Input element.
acceptanceCriteria:
WeeklyGoalSection.jsx error

has id='goal-error'
Input has aria-describedby='goal-error' when goalError is non-null
aria-describedby is omitted when there is no error
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add role='group' label to DashboardToolbar view toggle container
userStories.description: DashboardToolbar.jsx has a div containing List view and Kanban view toggle buttons (~lines 17-33). While both buttons have aria-pressed, the container has no group semantics. Screen readers read them as isolated buttons. Wrap the two buttons in a div with role='group' and aria-label='View mode' so assistive technologies understand they are a related set of toggle controls.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardToolbar.jsx view toggle container div has role='group' and aria-label='View mode'
Both child buttons retain their aria-label and aria-pressed attributes
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sr-only sort direction text to ApplicationListHeader ColumnHeader
userStories.description: ApplicationListHeader.jsx ColumnHeader (~lines 9-16) renders a raw Unicode arrow character (↑ or ↓) inside a when a column is actively sorted. These Unicode characters are not reliably announced by all screen readers. Replace the {sortOrder === 'asc' ? '↑' : '↓'} with {sortOrder === 'asc' ? '↑' : '↓'}{sortOrder === 'asc' ? ', ascending' : ', descending'} so screen readers announce the sort direction.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationListHeader.jsx ColumnHeader sort arrow span has aria-hidden='true'
A sr-only span follows with text ', ascending' or ', descending' matching sort direction
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to PublicTimeline TimelineRow decorative dots and connecting line
userStories.description: PublicTimeline.jsx TimelineRow (~lines 27-32) renders a decorative h-3 w-3 rounded-full border div (timeline dot) and a w-0.5 flex-1 bg-border div (connecting line) that are purely visual. These divs have no aria-hidden and screen readers may announce them as empty containers. Add aria-hidden='true' to both the timeline dot div and the connecting line div.
acceptanceCriteria: PublicTimeline.jsx timeline dot div has aria-hidden='true'
PublicTimeline.jsx connecting line div has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Fix PublicTimeline StagePill redundant aria-label and add dot aria-hidden
userStories.description: PublicTimeline.jsx StagePill (~lines 13-20) has aria-label={stage} on the outer span AND renders {stage} as visible text inside. When aria-label and text content match, the aria-label is redundant and can confuse some AT. Remove aria-label from the StagePill outer span (the text content already serves as accessible name). Also add aria-hidden='true' to the inner colored dot span (~line 18).
acceptanceCriteria: PublicTimeline.jsx StagePill outer span does not have aria-label
StagePill inner dot span has aria-hidden='true'
The visible stage text still appears and serves as the accessible name
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to decorative separator dots in PublicTimeline and CalendarEventDetail
userStories.description: PublicTimeline.jsx TimelineRow (~line 36) and CalendarEventDetail.jsx EventApplicationInfo (~line 43) both render a · as a visual separator. These decorative middot characters should be hidden from screen readers. Add aria-hidden='true' to both separator span elements.
acceptanceCriteria: PublicTimeline.jsx separator middot span has aria-hidden='true'
CalendarEventDetail.jsx separator middot span has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sr-only met/not-met state to RegisterForm password requirement items
userStories.description:
RegisterForm.jsx renders password requirements in a
    (~line 21). Each item shows a ✓ or ○ character inside aria-hidden='true', but the met/not-met state is never communicated to screen readers. Add a sr-only span after the requirement text: {met ? ' (met)' : ' (not met)'} inside each requirement list item so screen readers announce whether each criterion is satisfied.
acceptanceCriteria: RegisterForm.jsx each password requirement list item has a sr-only span with '(met)' or '(not met)' text
The sr-only state changes dynamically as the user types
Existing aria-hidden checkmark symbol is unchanged
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-busy to DashboardToolbar Export CSV button
userStories.description: DashboardToolbar.jsx Export CSV button (~line 44) shows a spinning Loader2 icon when isExporting is true, but the button has no aria-busy attribute. Screen readers do not know the button is in a loading state. Add aria-busy={isExporting} to the Export CSV Button element so assistive technologies announce the busy state.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardToolbar.jsx Export CSV Button has aria-busy={isExporting}
aria-busy is false (or omitted) when not exporting
Build passes with no errors
